text: The surgeon demonstrates port placements for the laparoscopic procedure. The gastrocolic omentum is opened using a ligature device, revealing the duodenum. The duodenum is then extensively mobilized using a ligasure device, reaching the level of the aorta.

The infrapancreatic superior mesenteric vein is identified, and the right gastroepiploic vein is double-clipped and divided. The common hepatic artery is visualized, with the gastroduodenal artery being isolated, clipped, and divided. The portal vein and common bile duct are exposed lateral to the common hepatic artery.

The surgeon dissects the lymph node chain along the proper hepatic artery up to the celiac axis and removes it. The cystic duct is identified, double clipped, and divided. The common hepatic duct is isolated with umbilical tape and divided above the cystic duct insertion. The distal end of the duct is controlled with a vicryl endo loop, while the proximal end is secured with a laparoscopic bulldog clamp.

The surgeon cross-staples the antrum of the stomach using two firings of a linear laparoscopic stapler. An intraoperative ultrasound confirms the pancreatic mass is clear of adjacent vessels. A retropancreatic window is created, and umbilical tape is passed to guide the transection of the pancreatic neck, which is performed using a ligature device.

The surgeon cross-staples and mobilizes the jejunum, delivering it to the right subhepatic space. The remaining attachments of the specimen to the superior mesenteric vein are carefully divided using a combination of ligature and hook cautery, allowing for the removal of the specimen.

The surgeon completes the hepaticojejunostomy using 3-0 Vicryl sutures on an SH needle. The pancreaticojejunostomy is constructed in two layers with 3-0 silk sutures on an RB needle, employing the duct mucosa technique. A clear view of the pancreatic duct is visible as the anastomosis begins.
The surgeon constructs the gastro-jejunostomy, followed by closing the common channel using two firings of the laparoscopic linear stapler.
